'''Justin Horo''' (born 7 September 1986) is a retired New Zealand professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|sr}}er and {{rlp|lk}} for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]]. He previously played for the [[Parramatta Eels]] and the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]].

==Background==
Born in [[Auckland, New Zealand]], he is of Maori descent. His father, [[Mark Horo]] is New Zealand Maori and his mother is of Laos descent. His uncle is [[Shane Horo]].

==Playing career==
Horo played his junior football for the [[St Clair Comets]] and Cambridge Park, playing SG Ball & Jersey Flegg at the Sydney Roosters before signing with the [[Parramatta Eels]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/unsung-horo-starting-to-have-a-ball-on-field-after-injuryplagued-seasons-20100722-10n25.html/|title=Unsung Horo starting to have a ball on field after injury-plagued seasons|website=www.smh.com.au}}</ref>

In round 3 of the [[2010 NRL season]] he made his [[NRL]] debut for the [[Parramatta Eels]] against the [[Wests Tigers]]. Later that year he re-signed with the Parramatta Eels on a three-year contract, knocking back offers from the [[Newcastle Knights]], the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] and the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]].<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/parramatta-eels-re-sign-eric-grothe-and-justin-horo/story-e6frexni-1225910578692|title=Parramatta Eels re-sign Eric Grothe and Justin Horo|newspaper=The Daily Telegraph|date=26 August 2010|first=Josh|last=Massoud}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/parramatta-cant-match-rivals-offers-for-horo-20100824-13qe5.html|title=Parramatta can't match rivals' offers for Horo|newspaper=Sydney Morning Herald|date=25 August 2010|first=Chris|last=Barrett}}</ref> After making his debut, Horo played in all the remaining games of the 2010 season and received the [[2010 Parramatta Eels season]]'s rookie of the year award.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/catalans-announce-justin-horo-signing/|title=Catalans announce Justin Horo signing|website=www.totalrl.com}}</ref>

Horo played throughout the [[2011 NRL season|2011 season]] but in [[2012 NRL season|2012]] he was dropped from the Parramatta team after a first round loss, and featured in just six games for the year. Horo said, "[[Stephen Kearney|Steve]] (Kearney) said I had a few things to work on and I ended up getting a few injuries after that. I tore ligaments in my ankle and then I had a rotator-cuff injury in my shoulder. Once I got back to full fitness I ended up stringing a few games together at the end but that was after [[Stephen Kearney|Mooks]] had gone."<ref>{{cite news|first=Paul|last=Crawley|work=Daily Telegraph|title=Parramatta Eels reject Justin Horo keen to fill Tony Williams' boots at Manly Sea Eagles|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/parramatta-eels-reject-justin-horo-keen-to-fill-tony-williams-boots-at-manly-sea-eagles/story-e6frexnr-1226590275502|accessdate=5 March 2013|date=4 March 2013}}</ref>

After being told he was not in Parramatta's future plans early in the 2012 season, Horo considered playing park football in [[France]] with a friend before ending up signing a two-year contract with the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] on 12 November from the [[2013 NRL season|2013 season]], after being released from the final year of his Parramatta contract.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.seaeagles.com.au/news-display/Sea-Eagles-sign-Horo-Tonga-and-Lawton/65843 |title=Sea Eagles sign Horo, Tonga and Lawton - Manly Sea Eagles |accessdate=2013-02-23 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20130317053418/http://www.seaeagles.com.au/news-display/Sea-Eagles-sign-Horo-Tonga-and-Lawton/65843 |archivedate=2013-03-17 }}</ref> In the 2013 season, he scored eight tries from 18 games (to round 20 including two byes), playing in the back row with representative forwards [[Anthony Watmough]] and [[Glenn Stewart]]. Horo played a total of 27 matches for Manly in the [[2013 NRL season]] including the [[2013 NRL Grand Final]] which Manly lost against the Sydney Roosters 26-18.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/oct/06/sydney-roosters-win-nrl-grand-final/|title=Sydney Roosters beat Manly Sea Eagles to take NRL grand final|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

On 6 August 2015, Horo signed a two-year contract to play in the [[Super League]] for French club the [[Catalans Dragons]] starting in 2016.<ref>{{cite news|title = Catalan (sic) Dragons sign Justin Horo for 2016 season|publisher = SkySports|date = 2015-08-06|url =  http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12216/9938773/catalan-dragons-sign-justin-horo-for-2016-season|accessdate = 2015-08-09}}</ref> In September 2017 he signed a two-year deal with [[Wakefield Trinity]]. Horo played with Wakefield Trinity until the end of 2019. In 2021, Horo spoke on the Locker Room podcast of his time at Wakefield disclosing that he didn't enjoy his stay at the club and was close to quitting after the first training session.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/41334232|title=Justin Horo: Wakefield Trinity sign Catalans Dragons second-rower|work=BBC Sport|date=20 September 2017|accessdate=17 January 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/justin-horo-wakefield-trinity-assessment-19887051/|title=Justin Horo's expletive-ridden assessment of his time at Wakefield Trinity won't endear him to fans|website=www.examinerlive.co.uk}}</ref>

==Representative career==
In 2010, Horo played for the [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]] team against [[England national rugby league team|England]].<ref name="NZ_Herald_10681035">{{cite news|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=10681035&ref=rss|title=League: Maori draw with England|date=16 October 2010|work=[[The New Zealand Herald]]|accessdate=8 October 2011}}</ref>

==Personal life==
Horo is from the [[Tainui]] iwi.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nzmrl.co.nz/team/JustinHoro.htm|title=Justin Horo|work=New Zealand Māori Rugby League|access-date=2011-01-20|archive-url=https://archive.today/20130414010825/http://www.nzmrl.co.nz/team/JustinHoro.htm|archive-date=2013-04-14|url-status=dead}}</ref> His father is former [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] international [[Mark Horo]] and he has stated his allegiance to the Kiwis.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/unsung-horo-starting-to-have-a-ball-on-field-after-injuryplagued-seasons-20100722-10n25.html|newspaper=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=23 July 2010|title=Unsung Horo starting to have a ball on field after injury-plagued seasons|first=Chris|last=Barett}}</ref> His uncle, [[Shane Horo]], also represented New Zealand. Horo recently competed in a high profile match race against multi-sport athlete Jordan Simi. Horo won the race but rumours that Horo had Jordan Simis part of the track watered pre-race to gain an advantage. Horo avoided conviction for a cocaine charge, after being caught by police with possession and a rolled up note, in a Sydney pub in early 2021. Horo currently runs a rugby league podcast called Levels Network alongside former player [[Willie Mason]].<ref>{{cite web| url = https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/truecrimeaustralia/police-courts-nsw/former-nrl-star-justin-horo-charged-with-cocaine-possession-at-double-bays-golden-sheaf/news-story/ef120de959c7f5cdc6cc6aa5ed516c61| title = Reference at www.dailytelegraph.com.au}} </ref>
